items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` **Service directory:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` **Triggered by:** PR #58483 by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed A new provisioning library `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` (1.0.0-beta.1) was introduced, adding declarative Azure provisioning support for Application Gateway for Containers resources: `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, `TrafficControllerAssociation`, and `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. ## Gaps Found - **"Authenticate the Client" section is empty** — the heading exists under "Getting started" but has no content; provisioning libraries should explain how to set up credentials (e.g., `DefaultAzureCredential` with `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` via the `azd` deployment workflow) - **"Key concepts" section lacks resource descriptions** — it contains one generic sentence about declarative provisioning but does not explain what the four resource types do (TrafficController/Application Gateway for Containers, Frontend, Association, SecurityPolicy) - **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` is undocumented in Examples** — the CHANGELOG lists it as a key feature but there is no example in the README - **"Next steps" section is empty** — the heading exists but has no content (links to AGfC documentation, tutorials, or related provisioning packages are missing)
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files **File to edit:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` Fill in the four gaps in the existing README. Do NOT restructure the file — only add content to the sections that are currently empty or thin. **1. "Authenticate the Client" section** — add after the `### Authenticate the Client` heading: ```markdown No explicit authentication is needed in this library. `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` generates Bicep/ARM templates that are deployed by [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Authentication to Azure is handled by `azd` at deployment time, typically via [`DefaultAzureCredential`]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` **2. "Key concepts" section** — replace or augment the single existing sentence with: ```markdown This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates. ### Resource types | Type | Description | |------|-------------| | `TrafficController` | Represents an [Application Gateway for Containers]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) resource — the top-level traffic controller. | | `TrafficControllerFrontend` | A frontend endpoint on a `TrafficController` that receives inbound traffic. | | `TrafficControllerAssociation` | Associates a subnet with a `TrafficController` so it can route traffic. | | `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` | A security policy resource that can be attached to a `TrafficController` to enforce WAF or other security rules. | ``` **3. "Examples" section** — add a second example after the existing "Create a Traffic Controller" snippet: ```markdown ### Create a Security Policy This example demonstrates how to create an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` and attach it to an existing `TrafficController`. ```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy Infrastructure infra = new(); TrafficController controller = new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01); infra.Add(controller); ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy policy = new(nameof(policy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01) { Parent = controller, }; infra.Add(policy); ` `` ` ``` > **Note:** add the `#region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy` / `#endregion` markers around the corresponding code in the test file (see Step 2 below) before running `Update-Snippets.ps1`. **4. "Next steps" section** — add after the `## Next steps` heading: ```markdown - [Application Gateway for Containers documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) quickstart]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. **Add a new snippet to** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` Add a second static helper method and `[Test]` that covers `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`, wrapping the infrastructure-building code in `#region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy` / `#endregion`. Reference the existing `CreateTrafficControllerTest()` method in the same file as a pattern for using `Trycep.Define`. 2. **Add the README placeholder** in the Examples section at the location noted in Step 1 above: ` ```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy ` ` ``` ` The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will replace the content between these fences with the actual code extracted from the test file. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` already contains all six required sections. After the changes above, confirm each section has substantive content: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the Client (needs content added) 2. ✅ **Key concepts** (needs resource-type descriptions added) 3. ✅ **Examples** (needs SecurityPolicy example added) 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — already has GitHub Issues and Stack Overflow links 5. ✅ **Next steps** (needs links added) 6. ✅ **Contributing** — already complete Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 servicenetworking` — check the git diff to confirm README was updated with the new snippet 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 servicenetworking` — no new public types were added in this change, but run to confirm no API listing drift 5. `dotnet format sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above ```
items.title: [ServiceNetworking] Docs: README gaps in new Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking package
